description:
You are a capacity-planning engineer who has just received two dated snapshots of raw resource-usage data that are already present on disk.  
Your job is to:

1. Organise a new working area at  
   /home/user/capacity_planner  
   (create it if it does not yet exist).

2. Inside that working area, create this exact directory tree (missing elements must be created):  
   /home/user/capacity_planner  
   ├── data  
   └── logs  

3. By using symbolic links only (do NOT copy or move the source files/directories), make the following links inside /home/user/capacity_planner/data  

   • latest_cpu.csv      → /home/user/snapshots/2023-07-01/cpu.csv  
   • latest_memory.csv   → /home/user/snapshots/2023-07-01/memory.csv  
   • legacy              → /home/user/snapshots/2023-06-01            (this one is a directory symlink, not a file symlink)

   The names must match **exactly** and the symbolic links must be created such that a plain `ls -l` in /home/user/capacity_planner/data shows the three items with `-> ABSOLUTE_PATH_TO_TARGET` (absolute path, no relative components like `..`).

4. Produce a concise verification log named  
   /home/user/capacity_planner/logs/symlink_report.log

   The automated tests will read this file.  
   It must satisfy **all** of the following formatting rules:

   a. Plain UTF-8 text, no colour codes, tabs, or extra spaces.  
   b. Exactly three lines, **no blank lines** at the beginning, middle, or end of the file.  
   c. Each line must follow this template (case-sensitive):  

      <link_name> -> <absolute_target>  

      • <link_name> is the file or directory name that exists in /home/user/capacity_planner/data  
      • A single space, a hyphen, a greater-than sign, and a single space (` -> `) separate the two fields.  
      • <absolute_target> is the full absolute path of the object the link points to (no trailing slash unless it is already part of the name above; for this task no trailing slash is required).  

   d. The lines must appear **in this exact order**:  

      1) latest_cpu.csv -> /home/user/snapshots/2023-07-01/cpu.csv  
      2) latest_memory.csv -> /home/user/snapshots/2023-07-01/memory.csv  
      3) legacy -> /home/user/snapshots/2023-06-01  

   e. A final newline (ASCII 0x0A) must terminate the last line of the file.

5. Verify your work locally any way you like (e.g. `readlink -f`, `ls -l`) but do not include these checks in the log.  
   The only file that the automated grader inspects is /home/user/capacity_planner/logs/symlink_report.log, and it will confirm that each symbolic link resolves to the correct absolute target.

Hints for planning (no obligation to follow this exact order):
• mkdir, ln -s, and echo/printf redirection are likely all that is required (≈ 5-10 commands).  
• Use absolute paths when creating symlinks to avoid accidental relative paths.  
• Remember to include the directory-symlink named “legacy”.  
• Double-check the spelling and case of every path in the log file.

Your deliverable is the correct set-up of symbolic links plus the accurately formatted log file. No other files must be modified or added in /home/user.
truth:
Initial pre-existing structure (already on disk when the task starts):

/home/user/snapshots
├── 2023-06-01
│   ├── cpu.csv        (content irrelevant to grader)
│   └── memory.csv
└── 2023-07-01
    ├── cpu.csv
    └── memory.csv

Expected final state for verification:

1. Symbolic links (file type “l”) that must exist after the agent finishes:

/home/user/capacity_planner/data/latest_cpu.csv  →  /home/user/snapshots/2023-07-01/cpu.csv
/home/user/capacity_planner/data/latest_memory.csv → /home/user/snapshots/2023-07-01/memory.csv
/home/user/capacity_planner/data/legacy → /home/user/snapshots/2023-06-01  (directory symlink)

The links must be absolute, not relative.

2. Verification log file:

Path: /home/user/capacity_planner/logs/symlink_report.log  
Permissions: any regular file readable by current user (e.g. 0644) is fine.  

Exact required content (including terminating newline):

latest_cpu.csv -> /home/user/snapshots/2023-07-01/cpu.csv
latest_memory.csv -> /home/user/snapshots/2023-07-01/memory.csv
legacy -> /home/user/snapshots/2023-06-01

<newline_at_end>

3. No other files under /home/user/capacity_planner/ will be checked or relied upon.

Automated tests:

• readlink -f check that each symlink resolves to the correct absolute path.  
• The log file is compared byte-for-byte (after normalising LF endings).
